nonBlockingIssues.description: Stale module docstring in text-editor/main.rs missing MSG_SELECTION_UPDATE (33), MSG_WRITE_DELETE_RANGE (34), MSG_CURSOR_MOVE (32)
cursor_bar_suppressed_with_selection test only verifies boolean logic, not actual pixel suppression
scroll_offset_preserved_across_context_switch test is trivially weak (saves u32, asserts it equals itself)
scroll_clips_lines_above_and_below test only checks line numbering, not actual pixel clipping
draw_tt_sel_scroll duplicates ~80 lines from draw_tt_sel — could delegate with scroll_offset=0
parse_svg_number does not support leading decimal point (e.g., '.5') — valid SVG but truncates to 0 anyway for integer coords
svg_coord_to_fp silently truncates i32 for coordinates > ~524,000 at 1× scale — safe for icon paths but undocumented
alloc_zeroed return value not null-checked before dereference (consistent with codebase pattern but UB on OOM)
Test svg_icon_doc_rasterizes_at_20x24 actually rasterizes at 24x28 — misleading name
Comment on MAX_DIRTY_RECTS implies pad field is overhead counted before rects; actual field ordering differs (cosmetic)

appliedUpdates.description: Added Editor ↔ Compositor IPC Protocol section to architecture.md documenting all 6 message types (MSG_KEY_EVENT, MSG_WRITE_INSERT, MSG_WRITE_DELETE, MSG_CURSOR_MOVE, MSG_SELECTION_UPDATE, MSG_WRITE_DELETE_RANGE)
Added SVG API documentation to Drawing Library section in architecture.md (svg_parse_path, svg_rasterize, palette module, SVG struct size warning)
Updated font pipeline shared buffer layout in architecture.md to include SVG icon data (mono | prop | PNG | SVG icon)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update test baseline from '719+ tests (698 system + 21 prototype)' to '847 tests (826 system + 21 prototype)' to reflect current state after all three milestones.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviewers (svg-renderer, polish-pass) noted the stale baseline number. Actual test count is 847.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance: 'When adding a new module to the drawing library, use the include!() macro pattern established in lib.rs (e.g., include!("my_module.rs")). This exports the module's items at the drawing:: namespace level.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: polish-pass reviewer noted the include!() pattern is established but undocumented. Future workers must discover it by reading lib.rs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add test quality guidance: 'Unit tests should verify actual behavior (pixel output, state changes), not just arithmetic identity. Test names should accurately describe what is being verified. Tests named after integration scenarios (e.g., context_switch_preserves_X) should test the actual mechanism, not a trivial proxy.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Scrolling feature had two tests (scroll_offset_preserved_across_context_switch, scroll_clips_lines_above_and_below) that only test arithmetic/line numbering, not the actual compositor behavior described in their names.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to os-worker skill's 'Important Constraints': 'SVG structures SvgPath (~16 KiB) and SvgRasterScratch (~64 KiB) exceed the 16 KiB userspace stack. When using the SVG renderer in bare-metal code, heap-allocate these via alloc_zeroed and free after use.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: svg-icons-in-chrome worker had to discover and solve a stack overflow by switching to alloc_zeroed + svg_parse_path_into(). This is a recurring trap for future SVG users.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker used inline Color::rgb/rgba constants rather than palette constants
rejectedObservations.reason: Already resolved — the polish-pass feature (later commit 2545c2e) centralized all colors. Not a worker error; the pattern wasn't established at the time.

rejectedObservations.observation: DirtyRect array size hardcoded in 3 locations rather than using MAX_DIRTY_RECTS constant
rejectedObservations.reason: Pre-existing pattern not introduced by any M3 feature. The misc-m1-cleanup feature already corrected the value from 7→6. Refactoring to use the constant everywhere is a future improvement, not a scrutiny finding.
